title: Rich Rustic French Lentil Gratin
description: A hearty and flavorful French dish featuring lentils and a crunchy crust, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is shojin-friendly and uses flax eggs as a substitute for traditional eggs.

ingredients.name: lentils
ingredients.notes: green or brown lentils work well

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot, saute the onion, carrots, and celery in olive oil until the vegetables are tender.
Add the lentils, tomato paste, mustard, and vegetable broth to the pot, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the lentil mixture for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
In a small bowl, mix the flax eggs with 4 tablespoons of water and let sit for 5 minutes to thicken.
In a separate bowl, mix the breadcrumbs and nutritional yeast.

In a 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of the lentil mixture, followed by a layer of the breadcrumb mixture, and repeat this process until all ingredients are used, ending with a layer of breadcrumbs on top.
Drizzle the flax egg mixture over the top layer of breadcrumbs and bake for 25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
Remove the gratin from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Deglaze the pot used for cooking the lentils with a little water to get the fond, and serve the gratin with the deglazed fond spooned over the top.
